task: I’ve got a file that needs to be sent later today, but I may not be at my desk when it’s time. What are a few practical ways to set things up ahead of time so the handoff still goes through if my location or connection changes?

turns.agent_response:
A practical setup is:

1. Finish the file now
- Save the final version locally.
- Make one dated backup copy.

2. Put the final file in one known place
- Use a single folder or location for the version you’ll send.
- Avoid keeping “final” copies in multiple spots.

3. Pre-write the handoff details
- Recipient
- Subject line
- Filename
- Any short note you want to include

4. Stage the send before you leave
- If it’s email, draft it and attach the file now.
- If it’s an upload, open the page and get it ready as far as you can.

5. Have a backup way to get online
- Phone hotspot
- Another network
- Nearby place with signal if needed

6. Verify the file once
- Open it and confirm it’s the right version before you walk away.

A simple rule: have everything ready except the final click. That way, if your location or connection changes, you only need to reconnect and send.

If you want, I can turn this into a short email-send checklist or an upload checklist.

user_simulator.text:
That’s about right. Clear enough to use. I’d probably add one thing: keep the file name plain and consistent, no last-minute guessing. I’ve had enough of that kind of mess.

The checklist version would be useful, especially for email.
